summary: Decent audience-sourced recording with a clear cassette‑to‑PCM transfer and an upfront stage perspective, though no musical details are given.
review: The tape originates from a cassette master captured close to the stage and was transferred in 1997 using a Kyocera D811 into a Sony PCM2600, resulting in an upfront sound that many listeners find preferable to other 44.1 kHz PCM versions on Archive.org. While no generational loss is evident and the lack of a Nakamichi 700ii suggests modest tape quality, the recording remains solid overall. No comments on the actual performance are provided, so musical assessment is unavailable.

ai_rating.rationale: Single positive review (4 stars) focuses on decent recording quality; no show information limits confidence.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cassette master transferred from Kyocera D811 to Sony PCM2600 in ~1997, no Nakamichi 700ii, no generational loss, closer to stage gives more upfront perspective.
